mnemonics: UI/UX & SE are like BUILDING A HOUSE: UX is the blueprint (plan), UI is the interior design (look/feel), and Software Engineering is the construction (building it).
keyPoints: UI/UX design provides the blueprint (wireframes, prototypes) that software engineers implement.
Close collaboration is essential to ensure the final product matches the design intent and provides a good user experience.
Software engineering practices (like version control and agile methodologies) are often integrated into the UI/UX workflow for better collaboration and iteration.
commonMistakes: Treating design and development as separate, siloed phases instead of a collaborative, iterative process.
Engineers implementing a design without understanding the user flow or intent, leading to a dysfunctional experience.
Designers creating visually appealing prototypes that are unrealistic or too expensive to build from an engineering perspective.
